description: This figure presents a comparative evaluation of the studied Fe35Mn6Al0.1C low-carbon low-density cryogenic steel against multiple reported alloys tested at liquid nitrogen temperature (LNT), using specific yield strength as the common abscissa. Panel (a) plots Charpy impact energy versus specific yield strength, whereas panel (b) plots total elongation versus specific yield strength. The "Present work" (red circle) is benchmarked against low-carbon and high-carbon lightweight steels, low-carbon high-manganese steel, TWIP steel, 304 stainless steel, 9Ni steel, and the Al0.1CoCrFeNi high-entropy alloy. At a specific yield strength near 70 MPa·cm³·g⁻¹, the designed steel exhibits a Charpy impact energy of approximately 250 J and a total elongation near 75%, which caption states correspond to 254 ± 6 J and 75.5 ± 0.5%. It surpasses high-carbon lightweight and TWIP steels in impact toughness, exceeds low-carbon high-Mn and 304 stainless steels in specific yield strength, and delivers ductility nearly three times higher than 9Ni steel. These data collectively demonstrate that the steel combines low density, excellent cryogenic toughness, superior ductility, and cost effectiveness, establishing it as a highly competitive candidate for cryogenic applications.
